mission: TOOL-ERRORS: Scope typed error taxonomy for .opencode/tools/*.ts

preexisting_findings_relevant.summary: Two tools in registry are placeholders (delegate.ts, session-diff.ts) — erodes trust in tool registry
preexisting_findings_relevant.relevance: These tools have no real implementation, so no errors to migrate

dual_tool_systems.observation: Two completely separate tool systems coexist with no error-type sharing

system_a.name: Built-in framework tools
system_a.error_model: 5 Schema.TaggedErrorClass subclasses (InvalidArgumentsError, ToolError, TimeoutError, TransientError, ValidationError)
system_a.runtime: Effect ecosystem with classifyError() dispatcher in typed-result.ts:135-195
system_a.signature: Effect<ExecuteResult, ToolError | TimeoutError | ...> — typed in Effect channel
system_a.consumer: processor.ts handles errors via Effect.catchTag + instanceof checks

system_b.name: User-defined config tools
system_b.signature: execute(args, context): Promise<string> — untyped, string output only
system_b.consumer: output is displayed as raw string to LLM; tool.execute.after hook in plugin Hooks interface can intercept

pattern_1_flat_error.characteristics: No status discriminator, no error code, freeform string message. Agent must parse JSON and check for 'error' key.
pattern_1_flat_error.migration_difficulty: low — add status field, error_code enum

pattern_2_status_error.characteristics: Has status discriminator but inconsistent values: 'error', 'fail', 'blocked', 'ambiguous'. Error message is still freeform string. Closest to structured but lacks error codes.
inconsistencies: smart_bash.ts uses status: 'error' for spawn failures vs status: 'blocked' for destructive commands — inconsistent with smart_sd.ts which uses status: 'fail' for similar spawn errors
smart_edit.ts uses status: 'ambiguous' for multi-match (unique to this file)
No file consistently maps status values to error categories

pattern_3_status_only.characteristics: Status indicates error but no error message, no error code. Agent must infer from status alone.

pattern_4_no_error_handling.pattern: No explicit error returns — implicit success or silent failures
files: task_board.ts (no error paths — assumes sessions dir exists)
analytics.ts (no error path — returns empty summary)
pattern_4_no_error_handling.characteristics: Silently succeeds or returns degraded output. No error classification possible without adding error paths.

unknown_action.canonical_pattern: { error: "Unknown action: 'X'. Valid: a, b, c." }

not_found.files: roadmap.ts, plan.ts, read_source.ts, read.ts, replace_symbol.ts, smart_edit.ts, smart_sd.ts, smart_find.ts, smart_write.ts, json_query.ts, gate.ts, verify.ts, smart_session.ts
not_found.canonical_pattern: Mixed: { error: 'X not found' } or { status: 'not_found', path: X }

internal_error.files: smart_bash.ts, smart_bun.ts, smart_git.ts, smart_grep.ts, smart_edit.ts, smart_sd.ts, smart_write.ts, smart_find.ts, smart_stats.ts, replace_symbol.ts, read.ts, smart_session.ts, json_query.ts
internal_error.canonical_pattern: { status: 'error', error: result.error.message } or { error: 'Parse error' }

permission_denied.files: smart_delegate.ts (2 sites: spawn rules, sync forbidden), smart_bash.ts (destructive commands), smart_git.ts (destructive operations)

conflict.files: plan.ts (already exists), file_lock.ts (already locked), fragment.ts (collision), smart_edit.ts (ambiguous text)

timeout.note: No tool explicitly handles timeout — spawnSync has timeout option but errors surface as generic spawn errors

transient.note: No explicit transient error handling — file I/O and spawn errors are treated as generic internal errors

key_consumer_locations.role: parseOutput() — locates [[typed-result]] marker, parses embedded JSON as TypedToolResult. Does NOT currently parse .opencode/tools/ output.
key_consumer_locations.impact: If we add typed-result markers to .opencode/tools/ output, this consumer would need updating.

key_consumer_locations.role: finishToolCall() — wraps tool output as ToolPart state. Currently takes raw output string and creates { status: 'error', error: new Error(output) }.
key_consumer_locations.impact: Would benefit from structured error parsing but is backward-compatible.

key_consumer_locations.role: Checks part.state.status === 'error' to detect failed tool calls. Also sets ToolStateError on subtask failures.
key_consumer_locations.impact: Depends on status field being 'error' — our current patterns already use this.

key_consumer_locations.role: Parses OpenAI-style error envelopes: { type: 'error', error: { type: 'too_many_requests' } }
key_consumer_locations.impact: If we adopt this envelope style, retry logic could automatically handle tool errors.

key_consumer_locations.role: classifyError() — centralized dispatch on Tool.* error classes via instanceof
key_consumer_locations.impact: Only works for Effect tool system (System A). User tools (System B) have no Error objects to classify.

backward_compatibility_assessment.adding_error_code: safe — adding a new field to existing JSON objects does not break consumers
backward_compatibility_assessment.changing_error_structure: risky — consumers that check for specific keys would break
backward_compatibility_assessment.adding_typed_result_marker: safe if opt-in per tool — only tools that add the marker get parsed
backward_compatibility_assessment.changing_status_values: risky — prompt.ts and processor.ts check for exact 'error' status value

shared_error_module_design.design_rationale: The plugin SDK provides no error types. A shared module within .opencode/tools/ is the natural boundary — tools import from it, and it can be versioned independently.

fields.hint: string? (optional remediation hint, already used by 10+ tools)

proposed_exports.name: isToolError
proposed_exports.purpose: Check if a tool output string contains an error

proposed_exports.name: parseToolError
proposed_exports.purpose: Parse a tool output string into a structured ToolErrorOutput

proposed_exports.name: makeError
proposed_exports.signature: (code: ErrorCode, message: string, opts?: { detail?, recoverable?, hint? }) => string
proposed_exports.purpose: Create a standardized error JSON string

phases.name: Create shared error module
phases.description: Create the errors.ts module with ErrorCode enum, ToolErrorOutput type, makeError() builder, and parseToolError() parser. No existing files changed.

phases.name: Add error code to tools using pattern_2 (status+error) — lowest hanging fruit
phases.risk: low — adding error_code field is purely additive, no consumer breaks
phases.description: Import { makeError, ErrorCode } from './errors'. Replace each { status: 'error', error: '...' } with makeError(ErrorCode.X, '...'). Existing status and error fields preserved for backward compatibility.

phases.name: Convert pattern_1 (flat error) tools to use status+error_code
phases.risk: medium — adding status field changes output shape, but the old 'error' key is preserved
phases.description: Replace { error: '...' } with { status: 'error', error_code: '...', error: '...', hint: '...' }. The 'error' key is preserved so consumers that check for it still work.

phases.name: Add typed-result markers for opt-in structured parsing
phases.total_edit_points: 4 (one per tool, wrapping the return value)
phases.risk: medium — adds [[typed-result]] marker that typed-result.ts:383 parses
phases.description: Wrap return value with [[typed-result]] marker so parseOutput() in typed-result.ts can extract structured errors. Opt-in per tool.

phases.name: Add error handling to tools with no error paths
files_to_modify: task_board.ts (add graceful degradation when sessions dir is missing)
phases.description: Add try/catch around I/O operations with structured error returns.

phases.name: Update consumers to parse structured errors
phases.risk: medium — changes session processing, needs integration tests
phases.description: Update finishToolCall() to parse error_code from tool output. Update classifyError() to handle ErrorCode enum.

smoking_guns.finding: Two completely separate error systems with zero sharing. The built-in tool framework has 5 typed Schema.TaggedErrorClass errors with classifyError() dispatcher, while .opencode/tools/ has 65 ad-hoc JSON string error sites across 30 files.
evidence: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts:23-64 defines InvalidArgumentsError, ToolError, TimeoutError, TransientError, ValidationError
.opencode/tools/*.ts all use JSON.stringify({ error: '...' }) or JSON.stringify({ status: 'error', error: '...' })

smoking_guns.finding: The plugin SDK (@opencode-ai/plugin) tool.execute() returns Promise<string> with NO error channel. There is no way to propagate typed errors through the plugin boundary.
evidence: No ToolResult type, no error field in output type
smoking_guns.mitigation: Error codes must be embedded in the JSON string output; the tool.execute.after hook in Hooks interface can intercept.

smoking_guns.finding: Inconsistent status values across tools: 'error', 'fail', 'blocked', 'ambiguous' are used interchangeably for similar error conditions.
evidence: smart_sd.ts uses status: 'fail' for spawn errors
smart_bash.ts uses status: 'error' for identical spawn errors
smart_edit.ts uses status: 'ambiguous' for multi-match (unique to this file)

smoking_guns.finding: 3 tools (task_board.ts, analytics.ts, github.ts) have no error paths at all. They silently succeed or return empty results even when I/O fails.
evidence: task_board.ts: no try/catch around readdirSync or readFileSync
analytics.ts: returns { sessions: [], summary: 'No sessions found' } without distinguishing 'empty' from 'error'

smoking_guns.finding: No timeout error handling exists in any .opencode/tools/ file. spawnSync has a timeout option but timeout errors surface as generic spawn errors indistinguishable from other failures.
evidence: smart_bash.ts:120: spawnSync({ timeout }) — error.message is generic 'ETIMEDOUT'

out_of_scope_findings_recorded.summary: Built-in tool framework errors (packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts) and user tool errors (.opencode/tools/) are completely disconnected — no shared taxonomy
out_of_scope_findings_recorded.recommended_slice: Unify error types via a shared package or re-export pattern
